description: Plugin-distributed hooks for ravenclaude-core. Auto-merged with the consumer's session hooks when the plugin is installed. Paths use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} so they resolve to the plugin's install location, not the consumer's project root.

hooks.comment: Auto-format files after agent edits. No-ops silently if no formatter is configured for the file type.

Advisory: warns when an edit to a plugin agent definition file looks like the agent is instructing itself to spawn another sub-agent — only the Team Lead dispatches. Set RC_GUARD_RECURSIVE_SPAWN_STRICT=1 to make this blocking.

Advisory (Claim Grounding & Source Honesty): nudges when an unhedged absolute capability claim ('you can't…', 'not possible') is written into a knowledge/ or docs/ markdown file without an inline provenance marker — a durable false prior the next session would trust. Opt-in (no-op unless the project has a .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml); skips frontmatter/code-fences/blockquotes/headings + conditional phrasings; honors a `claim-lint-ok` line escape. Exit 0 always; never blocks. Cannot see the chat answer (no hook can) — this is the durable-artifact surface only.

Advisory (Capability Grounding — 'Consult your access inventory before telling the user to check/do something'): nudges when a 'tell the user to manually check / open the portal / check the run history' phrasing is written into a knowledge/ or docs/ markdown file — a durable prior that teaches over-deference when the agent holds the route itself. Opt-in (no-op unless the project has a .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml); skips frontmatter/code-fences/blockquotes/headings; SUPPRESSES a genuine hand-back reason ('unless you lack…') or a line citing the held route (Web API/GET/pac/az/gh); honors a `delegation-nudge-ok` line escape. Exit 0 always; never blocks. HONEST LIMIT: cannot see the chat answer (the primary surface) — this is the written-artifact subset only; the inventory + the CGP clause are the real fix.

When a plugin.json or marketplace.json version changes inside the RavenClaude marketplace clone, regenerate the downstream artifacts that mirror that data (Copilot package, dashboard.html, repo-guide.html). Prevents the freshness gates from catching drift the author forgot to regenerate. No-ops outside the marketplace, on non-manifest edits, and when the version is unchanged. Failures are logged but never block.

Records the per-session first-use 'seen' marker for a YAML-whitelisted domain — but ONLY after the WebFetch actually proceeded (PostToolUse does not fire on a blocked/denied tool call). This is the consent-ordering complement to guard-web-access.sh's PreToolUse 'ask': a PreToolUse hook can't see its own ask's answer, so marking 'seen' there would silently auto-allow a domain the user just DENIED on the next retry. Recording consent post-allow here closes that inversion. Fail-safe: not WebFetch / no url / no jq -> no-op; never blocks.
Block obviously destructive commands that slipped past the consumer's deny list. Reads the tool call as JSON on stdin; exits 2 to block (exit 1 would not block).

Command review (the Thing) — T3 panel tribunal. No-ops (fast grep) unless a category has `thing: on` in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml. When a command's category is toggled on, convenes up to three reviewer seats in parallel (Forseti/Mímir/Heimdall) + Thor on a split, renders ALLOW/DENY/EDIT, writes a Sága-log audit entry, emits hookSpecificOutput (with updatedInput on EDIT). The internal panel deadline (~75s) stays under this 90s hook timeout so the script emits its own verdict before the platform's fail-OPEN-on-timeout fires. Fails closed (deny for high-stakes categories, ask for shell_readonly) on its own errors. Live for shell_readonly, shell_remote_mutate, shell_code_exec, shell_local_mutate, shell_package_install.

Runaway / rabbit-hole brake. Portable replacement for native auto-mode's 3/20 block brake (unavailable under Copilot CLI / multi-model). No-ops unless .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml exists; counts tool calls per session and exits 2 (block) when the agent thrashes (default 8 byte-identical calls in a row) or blows a generous total-call ceiling (default 1200). Tune under `runaway:` or disable with `runaway: off`. Deterministic, no model.
Layout enforcement. Reads .repo-layout.json from the consumer's project root; no-ops silently if the manifest is absent.

Worktree-hygiene guard (check). Reads the `worktree_guard: off|warn|block` knob from .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml (default warn when absent). On `block` DENIES (exit 2) ONLY a MUTATING op — git commit/add/checkout/switch/merge/rebase/cherry-pick/revert/stash/rm/mv, or a Write/Edit/MultiEdit under the working tree — and only when this session is contending with another live session in the same tree or is anchor-work; never a read / git status / rcwt. On `warn` (default) it emits a throttled (1/session/clause) stderr nudge and never blocks. Escape hatch: RC_WORKTREE_GUARD_ACK=1. `off` short-circuits before any git shell-out. Fail-open on missing git/jq; emits a hook-event audit line on a verdict.

Website allow/deny enforcement. Reads .ravenclaude/web-access.yaml (+ the per-session allow file): a whitelisted domain auto-allows with no prompt, a blacklisted domain is blocked (exit 2), an unlisted domain falls through to the normal per-domain prompt. Fail-safe: absent config / missing jq -> no-op (ask as normal). The deterministic backstop for the agent-surfaced once/session/permanent/deny choice.

Decision-review enforcement (the Thing, decision mode). No-ops (fast grep) unless `decision_review: advisory|binding` is set in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml (default off). When opted in AND the AskUserQuestion is a single, binary, non-multiselect yes/no-shaped question, routes it through thing-decide.py; on a BINDING yes/no verdict it DENIES the prompt with the verdict + reasoning so the agent proceeds without interrupting the user. Defer / advisory / high-blast / low-confidence / any error -> allow (the human answers). Every routed decision is Saga-logged under .ravenclaude/runs/thing/decisions/.

Definition-of-done gate. No-ops unless `definition_of_done.cmd` is set in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml. When set AND source changed this session, runs the gate command (tests/build/lint) at Stop and BLOCKS the stop (decision:block) until it passes — closing the 'looks done != is done' hallucination gap. Self-limited to max_blocks (default 8) consecutive blocks so it can't deadlock. Deterministic; ports to Copilot via the adapter's stop mode.

If any source files changed this session, remind the Team Lead to run the full test suite before reporting done. Advisory fallback when no definition_of_done.cmd is configured (dod-gate.sh no-ops in that case).

Agentic Work-Streams (P1). On session end, if a work-stream is active in .ravenclaude/streams/, append ONE derived session_closed event + refresh that stream's state.md resume snapshot (crash-resilience). DERIVED-ONLY (session_id FK + counts; never prompt text). Fail-safe: no python3 / no active stream / any error -> silent no-op, never blocks the stop.

Agentic Work-Streams (P4 — OPT-IN, FAIL-OPEN). No-ops (a single grep, zero cost) unless .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml sets `stream_hook: per_prompt` (default session_boundary). When opted in: if a work-stream is ACTIVE, attribute the prompt to it as ONE derived event (sticky — does NOT re-classify); if none is active AND stream_classify is `auto`, classify + set-active on a confident match. NEVER blocks/alters the prompt (always exit 0); a latency budget (RC_STREAM_HOOK_BUDGET_S, default 3s) caps the inner work. NO-EGRESS: only derived labels/terms/word-count are persisted (stream-ops refuses raw-content fields) — the prompt text never reaches disk.

Agent-dispatch evaluator (Phase 3 — AUDIT-ONLY). No-ops (single grep) unless .ravenclaude/dispatch-config.json has `enabled: true` (default false). When opted in, fires a Haiku classifier on the about-to-start subagent and SHADOW-LOGS a model-right-sizing verdict to hook-events.jsonl + .ravenclaude/runs/dispatch-eval/ — it NEVER denies or redispatches. Ships audit-only because SubagentStart fires post-spawn (RM1): a DENY may be a late cancel, not a pre-dispatch intercept, and this session could not verify deny-is-pre-commit live. The Phase-2 workflow wrapper (evaluate-dispatch.js) is the sole binding path. Fail-open on missing jq/claude/timeout.

Regenerates the comfort-posture project-layer rules from .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml at session start so the dashboard-composed posture is active without a manual /set-posture. No posture file -> silent no-op. Always exits 0; never blocks. This is the load-bearing wiring for the web flow, where only the committed project layer survives a fresh container.

Advisory: warns when a session loads in acceptEdits/bypassPermissions mode, which bypasses the comfort-posture allow/ask/deny rules. The real pin is permissions.defaultMode=default (written by /set-posture). Reads permission_mode from stdin; never blocks.

Injects a session-start capability banner via hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext so the agent opens every session aware of the project's external surface, its detected auth (env-var NAMES/presence only — never values), and its effective .claude/settings.json permissions. Local + cheap; the ONLY subprocess is a bounded, read-only git read (branch + recent commit subjects) used by the work-streams classifier, and only when .ravenclaude/streams/ has streams + none is active. A 10s timeout bounds any pathological config/git read (defense-in-depth). Points to the environment-discovery skill for deep enumeration. Fail-silent; never blocks (SessionStart hooks cannot). Salience boost for the Capability Grounding Protocol, not enforcement.

Worktree-hygiene guard (register). Records this session's own registry file under ${RC_WORKTREE_GUARD_HOME:-$HOME/.ravenclaude/worktree-guard} and GC-sweeps its PATH_KEY bucket (never depends on Stop firing). Reads the `worktree_guard: off|warn|block` knob from .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml (default warn when absent). On `warn`/`block` emits a SessionStart nudge only on real CONTENTION (another live session already operating in this same working tree) or ANCHOR-WORK (this is the primary checkout, worktrees already exist, and HEAD is on the anchor branch); a lone checkout in a fresh container satisfies neither and stays silent. `off` short-circuits before any git shell-out. Fail-open on missing git/jq; SessionStart cannot block (register only records intent). Emits a hook-event audit line on a verdict.
